title: Flood Trauma in Ghana: Mental Health Effects Can Linger Long After the Water Recedes – Psychiatrist Warns
summary: Korle Bu psychiatrist Dr. Albert Sedohia explains why flood survivors in Ghana may face anxiety, fear and grief long after the floodwaters recede — and shares warning signs, coping steps, and where to get help.

title: Floods Could Trigger Cholera and Typhoid Outbreaks – Dr. Joyce Emefa Addo- Klah Warns Ghanaians
summary: Accra, Ghana – As communities across Accra continue to recover from the devastating floods that followed Monday's heavy rains, medical practitioner Dr. Joyce Emifa has urged Ghanaians to take extra precautions to protect themselves from disease outbreaks that often follow flooding.

title: Flood Victims with Comprehensive Insurance Can File Claims – Insurance Expert Advises Ghanaians
summary: Following the devastating floods that swept through parts of Accra, insurance expert Hanna Ama Koomson, Managing Director of Guard Risk Limited, has assured affected policyholders that those with comprehensive insurance cover may be entitled to compensation for flood-related losses.

title: Rain Could Not Stop the Praise: Thousands Worship at Joe Mettle's Praise Reloaded 2026
summary: The Accra Sports Stadium became a sanctuary of worship on Sunday evening as thousands of Christians from across Ghana and beyond gathered for the 2026 edition of Praise Reloaded, the annual worship experience hosted by gospel minister Joe Mettle.

title: Heavy Rains Trigger Flooding Across Accra, Disrupting Traffic and Daily Activities
summary: Heavy rains that swept across Accra in the early hours of Monday have caused widespread flooding in several parts of the capital, leaving commuters stranded, disrupting businesses and once again raising concerns over the city's drainage system.

title: Joe Mettle Invites Christians to Gather to Lift Jesus at Praise Reloaded 2026
summary: Renowned gospel minister Joe Mettle has extended a heartfelt invitation to Christians across Ghana and beyond to attend Praise Reloaded 2026, scheduled for Sunday, June 28, at the Accra Sports Stadium.

title: She Couldn't Cook. She Lost Her Job. 18 Years Later, They're Still Standing.
summary:
It started with a social media post.
Evangeline wasn't looking for attention. She simply listed honestly, without polish 18 challenges she and Isaac had walked through together. The post took off. Nearly 50,000 views. Hundreds of comments from people who recognised their own marriage in it.
That post brought the couple to Sunny FM's IM Drive, where they told the fuller story.

title: Youth Alpha Leaders Training Set for the Dan Peter's Memorial Conference Room
summary: Young Christian leaders, youth workers, pastors, and volunteers will have a unique opportunity to deepen their understanding of evangelism and discipleship as Alpha Ghana, in collaboration with Sunny 88.7 FM, hosts a Youth Alpha Leaders Training session on Friday, July 3, from 10:00 a.m. to 1:00 p.m. at the Dan Peters Memorial Conference Room.

title: Everyday Faith, Global Purpose: Lausanne Launches Free Discipleship Video Series
summary: TheLausanne Movement has released a free five-part video series designedto help Christians connect personal faith with global mission,according to the organization.

title: Why Parental Mental Health Matters More Than Ever
summary: When we talk about mental health, we rarely put parents at the center of the conversation — yet they may need that spotlight most. A recent discussion on Cradle to Crayon, hosted by Abigail Kontoh on Sunny 88.7 fm, brought this often-overlooked issue to the fore, exploring how the mental well-being of parents shapes the health and happiness of the entire family.

title: Darbaa Borehole Project Set to Transform Lives Through Access to Clean Water
summary: Residents of Darbaa and its surrounding communities could soon experience a significant improvement in their quality of life as support continues to grow for the Darbaa Borehole Project.

title: Alpha Ghana Invites Churches to Rediscover the Power of Relational Evangelism
summary: As churches across Ghana seek fresh and effective ways to share the Gospel, the Alpha Course continues to emerge as one of the world's most impactful tools for evangelism and discipleship.
